canonical_text: The standard model of AI — optimizing a fixed objective — is fundamentally dangerous at scale
subject: Stuart Russell
verdict: Validated by reward hacking in RLHF and jailbreak exploits in frontier models

canonical_text: Superintelligent AI poses existential risk and should be taken seriously by the field
subject: Stuart Russell
verdict: Now consensus at major labs — OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepMind all have safety teams

canonical_text: AI systems should be designed to be uncertain about human preferences and defer to humans
subject: Stuart Russell
verdict: RLHF and constitutional AI embody this principle; Russell's framing was influential

canonical_text: Alignment must be solved before superintelligence is built
subject: Stuart Russell
verdict: Industry is building first, aligning second — warning unheeded but not proven wrong
